retained_observations.retained_reason: The active action matrix now mixes release authority, artifact freshness, fixture cost, compatibility alias, and external-verification work; closing this observation before lane sequencing would hide the coordination risk.
retained_observations.next_action: Use the active matrix priority order to pick one lane at a time and refresh the matrix/currentness evidence after each completed lane.
retained_observations.exit_condition: Close when the active matrix no longer needs a separate priority coordination note because the remaining active actions have current owner lanes and blocking scopes.

retained_observations.retained_reason: Compatibility alias cleanup is intentionally incremental because removing flat imports without caller inventory would turn a maintenance lane into a broad interface migration.
retained_observations.next_action: Update the compatibility-alias inventory on the next touched hotspot and migrate only verified callers in that ownership boundary.
retained_observations.exit_condition: Close when the compatibility-alias inventory has no remaining flat import re-export entries or each remaining alias has an explicit retained support contract.

retained_observations.retained_reason: This is the umbrella execution guard for the current long-term active-matrix cleanup; closing it would encourage new narrative reports instead of lane work.
retained_observations.next_action: Continue burning down the existing active lanes through code, tests, currentness artifacts, and release/supply-chain evidence instead of adding new planning reports.
retained_observations.exit_condition: Close when the active matrix blockers are either resolved, explicitly operator-dependent, or represented by narrower retained observations.
